purpose: Govern how final replies reflect memory load, writeback, skipped updates, and residual gaps for Meta-Runtime-Memory.
instruction: If memory was updated, the final reply should reflect the outcome at a high level.
If writeback was skipped, the final reply may mention that no durable signal was produced when relevant.
If a gap remains because the signal is still uncertain, call out the uncertainty instead of persisting it.
Do not dump raw memory payloads into the user-facing answer unless explicitly requested.
workflow: Check whether the turn loaded compiled memory successfully.
Check whether user memory or task memory changed.
Check whether turn delta was appended.
Summarize the outcome only to the extent that it matters for the current user task.
rules: Final reply is not a full memory report by default.
Memory persistence status may be summarized briefly.
Residual ambiguity should remain explicit rather than silently written back.
